title: Wire extra-packages.yaml as Docker build source of truth
summary: Review #2 external feedback addressed: Dockerfile's apt-get install for EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES is now wrapped in a conditional guard (`if [ -n "${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES}" ]`) so local builds without --build-arg don't fail with a cryptic 'E: No packages specified' error. A review pattern was documented for future detection. All 13 verify_release_config.sh tests pass.

tests_checklist.item: Edge case: empty EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES handled by conditional guard
Edge case: empty PIP_EXTRAS handled by inline conditional (line 22)
Edge case: empty PIP_PACKAGES handled by shell word splitting (no extra args)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Dockerfile patterns (SPDX header, DEVIATION comment, stage structure)
Comment explains WHY the guard exists, not just WHAT it does
Minimal change — only the apt-get RUN instruction was modified
No hardcoded package names in Dockerfile

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in Dockerfile
Unquoted ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} is intentional for word splitting; package names validated by CI test (dpkg pattern)

architecture_checklist.item: Guard pattern is the simplest solution (if/fi block vs alternatives like set +e or dummy package)
Consistent with PIP_EXTRAS guard on line 22 (both handle empty defaults gracefully)

fail_fast_checklist.item: CI still fails fast on missing/null pip_extras or apt_packages (workflow null-checks unchanged)
Local builds degrade gracefully (skip apt-get instead of cryptic error)

defensive_checklist.item: Empty EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES produces no-op instead of build failure
Other Dockerfiles (python-base, venv-builder) use hardcoded lists — guard not needed there
